rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar, Huber, and Khan (2007) to support a specific theoretical claim: an initial choice can change later preferences, which can shape subsequent behavior. This citation appears in the core conceptual framing of why post-failure behavior should be treated as distinct from the initial failure, and it is reiterated in the general discussion. Although the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's exact paradigm, the cited work meaningfully informs the conceptual setup of the new scale and argument.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper as prior evidence that earlier consumption choices can alter later preferences in sequential decisions. That evidence helps ground the citing paper's argument that post-failure behavior has distinct drivers and thus warrants a separate response-to-failure construct.
